description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by three dark metal hoops—one at the top, one at the bottom, and one around the middle. The wood has a rich, natural brown color with visible grain patterns, giving it a rustic and weathered appearance. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made from the same wood as the sides, tightly fitted to prevent spillage. The metal hoops are matte black and provide structural reinforcement, ensuring the barrel maintains its shape under pressure. There are no visible handles, spouts, or markings on the exterior, and the surface finish is rough and unpolished. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, storage areas, or near garages, often used for storage or as a decorative element.
description: A rustic wooden barrel with dark metal hoops, flat wooden top and bottom, and a rough, natural finish.
description_view_0: The barrel is seen from a slightly angled front, showing the wood grain and all three metal hoops clearly.
description_view_1: This view highlights the curvature of the barrel and the even spacing of the metal hoops, with the wood grain running vertically.
description_view_2: The barrel appears nearly identical to other views, with the wood texture and metal bands prominent and no visible openings or handles.
description_view_3: Similar to other angles, this view emphasizes the continuous wood staves and the secure fit of the top and bottom surfaces.
